AI River Surveillance

Live
Eco-Tech Hackathon 2026 • Team AlphaVerse

About NodiWatch

An AI-powered satellite surveillance system for protecting Bangladesh's 1,400+ rivers from pollution, encroachment, and erosion.

Our Mission

To leverage cutting-edge satellite technology and artificial intelligence to create an automated, transparent, and actionable monitoring system that empowers authorities to protect Bangladesh's vital river ecosystems.

Our Vision

A future where every river in Bangladesh is continuously monitored, violations are detected in real-time, and environmental accountability is strengthened through satellite-based evidence.

The Problem We're Solving

Industrial Pollution

Untreated industrial effluent from textile and tannery industries turns rivers toxic. Dissolved oxygen drops below 0.5 mg/L - lethal for aquatic life.

River Encroachment

Illegal land filling has shrunk river widths by up to 60% in some areas, displacing communities and increasing flood risk.

Bank Erosion

Riverbank erosion accelerating at 15m/year in critical zones, putting thousands of structures and lives at risk.

Meet the Team

A

Ahmmad Nur Swapnil

AI/ML Lead

Engineered CNN and Random Forest models for pollution and encroachment detection. Developed spectral analysis algorithms and TensorFlow training pipeline. Built erosion forecasting system using historical satellite data.

T

Tamim Hasan Saad

Full-Stack Developer

Built React/Next.js dashboard with real-time monitoring. Integrated PostGIS for spatial queries. Developed Green Banking API for carbon credits. Implemented alert system with SMS/email notifications.

E

Ekramul Haque Amin

Backend + Signal Processing Expert

Architected Google Earth Engine workflows for satellite data processing. Developed SAR analysis tools for erosion monitoring. Built backend infrastructure and data pipelines for real-time geospatial analysis.

H

Habiba Rafique

AI/ML + Database Expert

Trained machine learning models for threat classification. Optimized PostGIS database for spatial queries. Built data ingestion pipeline for processing satellite imagery and sensor data at scale.

S

Sonia Khatun

Full-Stack Developer

Developed frontend components with React/Next.js for data visualization. Implemented UI/UX features for evidence submission system. Integrated API endpoints for real-time alerts and monitoring dashboard.

Technology Stack

Sentinel-2
High-resolution optical imagery
Sentinel-1 SAR
All-weather radar monitoring
Google Earth Engine
Cloud-based analysis platform
TensorFlow
Machine learning framework
Gemini AI
Vision & chat capabilities
Next.js 14
React framework for the web
System Architecture

How NodiWatch Works

End-to-end pipeline from satellite acquisition to actionable intelligence

Spectral Fingerprinting

92%

Identify pollution types from spectral signatures at 10m resolution

Water Segmentation

86%

CNN-based river boundary detection with temporal differencing

Factory Attribution

78%

Bayesian inference links discharge events to industrial sources

Erosion Forecasting

83%

SAR coherence analysis predicts bank stability 72h in advance

Citizen Science

Community Ground-Truth

NodiWatch empowers citizens to become environmental monitors. Upload photos from your smartphone and our Gemini AI instantly analyzes them to validate satellite observations and create legally-admissible evidence.

AI-powered image analysis in seconds
GPS-tagged evidence with timestamp verification
Community validation for increased reliability
Submit Evidence

Target Stakeholders

Department of Environment (DoE)Bangladesh Water Development Board (BWDB)Bangladesh Inland Water Transport Authority (BIWTA)Environmental CourtsResearch OrganizationsNGOs & Civil Society

Eco-Tech Hackathon 2026

NodiWatch is developed as part of Phase 2 of the Eco-Tech Hackathon 2026, focusing on innovative solutions for environmental monitoring and protection.
